name: list_drafts
description: List Typefully drafts with optional status filter and limit. Use this to view your existing drafts before editing, scheduling, or deleting them. For retrieving a single draft's full details, use get_draft instead.

status.description: Filter by status: 'draft', 'scheduled', or 'published'. Default: all statuses

limit.description: Maximum number of drafts to return. Default: 10

name: create_draft
description: Create a new Typefully draft for social media posts. Supports single tweets, threads (separated by \n---\n), and multi-platform posts (X, LinkedIn, Threads, Bluesky, Mastodon). Use this to draft new content before scheduling. For scheduling existing drafts, use schedule_draft instead.

text.description: The text content of the draft. For threads, separate posts with '\n---\n'. Use '-' to read from stdin.

thread.description: Whether this is a thread (multiple posts). Default: false

schedule.description: Schedule time in ISO8601 format (e.g., '2026-03-01T09:00:00Z') or 'next-free-slot'. If not provided, the draft remains unscheduled.

name: get_draft
description: Get full details of a single Typefully draft by its ID. Use this to view complete draft information including content, scheduling status, and platform details before editing or scheduling. For listing multiple drafts, use list_drafts instead.

draft_id.description: The unique identifier of the draft to retrieve

name: edit_draft
description: Update an existing Typefully draft's content. Supports editing single posts or threads. Use this to modify draft text, convert to/from thread format, or change target platforms. For creating new drafts, use create_draft instead.

draft_id.description: The unique identifier of the draft to edit

text.description: New text content. For threads, separate posts with '\n---\n'

thread.description: Whether this is a thread. Default: false

name: schedule_draft
description: Schedule a Typefully draft for publication or publish immediately. Use this to set a specific publish time or use 'now' for immediate publishing. Use 'next-free-slot' to let Typefully's queue schedule it optimally. For creating drafts with scheduling in one step, use create_draft with --schedule instead.

draft_id.description: The unique identifier of the draft to schedule

schedule_time.description: Schedule time: ISO8601 format (e.g., '2026-03-01T09:00:00Z'), 'next-free-slot', or 'now' for immediate publishing

name: delete_draft
description: Delete a Typefully draft permanently. Use this to remove unwanted drafts. Use get_draft first to verify the draft ID if unsure.

draft_id.description: The unique identifier of the draft to delete

name: list_social_sets
description: List all available social sets (connected accounts) on Typefully. Use this to find your social set ID if you have multiple accounts connected. The script auto-detects your social set if only one account exists.
